prompt: Grill me on communicating a community-program result. The public decision is whether to call it causal proof or promising but inconclusive feedback; JSON/YAML storage and a route label are implementation details.
must: anchor the question in the public claim community members can rely on
ask only whether the public summary can call the result promising rather than causal proof
finish as soon as the stated public-decision scope is resolved
must_not: follow a locally interesting detail that no longer serves the mainline
invent an adjacent decision after the stated scope is resolved
repeat a status ribbon on every turn
ask whether to use JSON or YAML
ask how to name an internal route label
mainline_anchor: what public claim community members can rely on
question_anchor: public summary say the result is promising feedback, not proof that the program caused the improvement
evidence: Offline authored mainline-versus-implementation contrast based on the reported Grill drift. It checks fixture anchors only and does not prove live model behavior.
